instructions.instruction: Always use parseAsync() for async action handlers (not parse())
instructions.original_snippets: Use `parseAsync()` for async action handlers...NEVER add `.parseAsync()` without `await`...Commander.js v8+ requires `await program.parseAsync()` for async actions; without await, the process exits before async actions complete.
instructions.relevant_when: Building any CLI with async operations (file I/O, network requests, etc.)

instructions.instruction: Always await program.parseAsync() — never call it without await
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER add `.parseAsync()` without `await`...BAD: `program.parseAsync(process.argv)` without await. GOOD: `await program.parseAsync(process.argv)` inside an async IIFE or main function.
instructions.relevant_when: Finishing any CLI entry point file

instructions.instruction: Always pass the complete typed options object to services, never individual properties
instructions.original_snippets: ALWAYS pass complete typed options objects to services (never individual properties)...NEVER pass individual option properties to services (pass complete typed object)...DON'T pass options piecemeal (e.g., service(opts.a, opts.b, opts.c))
instructions.relevant_when: Calling any service or business logic function from an action handler

instructions.instruction: Define TypeScript interfaces for all options (e.g. BuildOptions, DeployOptions)
instructions.original_snippets: Define TypeScript interfaces for all options (e.g., `BuildOptions`, `DeployOptions`)...Always pass typed options objects to downstream services. Never access options piecemeal.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing TypeScript CLI commands with options

instructions.instruction: Export commands as Command instances from subcommand modules
instructions.original_snippets: Export commands as Command instances from subcommand modules...Export Command instances from subcommand modules
instructions.relevant_when: Building a multi-command CLI with subcommands

instructions.instruction: Use .addCommand() to attach subcommands to the parent Command
instructions.original_snippets: Use `.addCommand()` to attach subcommands to parent Command
instructions.relevant_when: Assembling a CLI with multiple subcommands in the main entry point

instructions.instruction: Create barrel exports for commands in commands/index.ts
instructions.original_snippets: Create barrel exports for commands (commands/index.ts)...Create barrel exports in `commands/index.ts`
instructions.relevant_when: Building a modular multi-command CLI

instructions.instruction: Use kebab-case for option names in CLI flags (not camelCase)
instructions.original_snippets: Use kebab-case for option names...DON'T use camelCase in CLI flags (use kebab-case)...Consistent Naming: kebab-case for flags: --output-dir not --outputDir
instructions.relevant_when: Defining options with multi-word names

instructions.instruction: Wrap action handlers in try/catch and call program.error() for Commander-formatted error output
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `.action()` callback without handling errors...Wrap in try/catch and call `program.error(err.message)` for Commander-formatted error output.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing any action handler that performs async or risky operations

instructions.instruction: Never access process.argv directly when Commander.js is available; define all arguments as Commander.js options or arguments
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER access `process.argv` directly when Commander.js is available...Commander.js handles argument parsing, validation, and help generation; bypassing it for any argument creates inconsistency in error handling and help output.
instructions.relevant_when: Any code that needs to read CLI input

instructions.instruction: Never use program.opts() inside a subcommand's action to read that subcommand's options; use the action's options parameter instead
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `program.opts()` to read option values inside a subcommand...Each subcommand has its own option scope; reading `program.opts()` in a subcommand returns the parent options, not the subcommand options.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing action handlers for subcommands that have their own options

instructions.instruction: Load version from package.json rather than hardcoding it
instructions.original_snippets: Always load version from package.json to keep it synchronized...Always load version from package.json to maintain single source of truth...Avoid - requires manual updates: program.version('1.2.3');
instructions.relevant_when: Setting up the CLI program version

instructions.instruction: Use .exitOverride() for testing to throw instead of calling process.exit
instructions.original_snippets: Use `.exitOverride()` for testing...For testing, override default exit behavior: program.exitOverride();
instructions.relevant_when: Writing tests for a Commander.js CLI

instructions.instruction: Provide sensible defaults for optional options
instructions.original_snippets: Provide sensible defaults for optional options...Don't make all options required (use sensible defaults)...Sensible Defaults: Provide defaults for optional options when sensible
instructions.relevant_when: Defining CLI options

instructions.instruction: Provide clear descriptions for all commands and options
instructions.original_snippets: Provide clear descriptions for all commands/options
instructions.relevant_when: Defining any command or option

instructions.instruction: Use .requiredOption() for options that must be provided rather than validating in action handlers
instructions.original_snippets: Use `requiredOption` instead of validating in action handlers
instructions.relevant_when: Defining options that are mandatory

instructions.instruction: Use choices() for options with a fixed set of valid values
instructions.original_snippets: Use `choices()` for enum values
instructions.relevant_when: Defining options that accept only specific values (e.g. log levels, environments)

instructions.instruction: Don't use global state in action handlers; use closures or pass state
instructions.original_snippets: Don't use global state in action handlers...Avoid Global State: use closures or pass state
instructions.relevant_when: Writing action handlers that need shared configuration or context

instructions.instruction: Organize modular CLIs with types/, services/, and commands/ directories following the types→services→commands architecture
instructions.original_snippets: types/ → Define option interfaces; services/ → Business logic (receives typed options); commands/ → Commander wiring (passes typed options to services)...Directory Structure with Service Layer
instructions.relevant_when: Building a substantial multi-command CLI application

instructions.instruction: Specify option argument types explicitly using <required> vs [optional] angle/square bracket syntax
instructions.original_snippets: Don't forget to specify option argument types (`<required>` vs `[optional]`)
instructions.relevant_when: Defining options that take values
